summary: PR #2417 is a small tooling fix for macOS builds. It changes tools/project.py so use_wibo only returns true on Linux, removing darwin from the automatic wibo path. The PR body says macOS builds were trying to execute build/tools/wibo instead of using wine, causing '/bin/sh: build/tools/wibo: cannot execute binary file'; the after log shows the progress command completing.

key_files.role: Adjusted compiler wrapper selection by restricting use_wibo platform detection to Linux only.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: def use_wibo changed from sys.platform == "linux" or sys.platform == "darwin" to sys.platform == "linux"
pr_body: macOS build tried to use wibo in place of wine

what_changed.text: In tools/project.py, use_wibo no longer treats sys.platform == "darwin" as eligible for wibo; it now requires sys.platform == "linux" while keeping the existing wibo_tag, machine architecture, and wrapper checks.
The PR body documents the observed failure on macOS: the build command invoked build/tools/wibo and failed with 'cannot execute binary file'.
The PR body includes an after log showing a completed PROGRESS step, but the slice does not include CI output, reviews, or independent validation beyond that pasted log.

smart_moves.text: The fix is narrow: it changes only the platform predicate for wibo selection and leaves architecture filtering and explicit wrapper override behavior intact.
The PR demonstrates the failure with the exact command line and shell error, making the platform-wrapper mismatch easy to diagnose.

decomp_lessons.text: Tooling changes can directly affect decomp workflow even when no source-matching code changes are involved; wrapper auto-detection should be treated as build-critical infrastructure.

follow_up_queries: macOS build wibo wine cannot execute binary file

notes: The changed file and exact one-line diff are fully present.
The PR body provides before and after command output, including the macOS failure mode and a successful progress output.
There are no review comments, issue comments, or reviews in the slice.
merged_at is null and the PR state is closed, so this intake does not claim the PR was merged or accepted.
The after log is author-provided PR evidence, not independent CI or regression verification.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Keep wrapper auto-detection platform-specific
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2417 fixed a macOS build failure by removing darwin from use_wibo auto-selection in tools/project.py; automatic wibo use was restricted to Linux so macOS would not try to execute build/tools/wibo.

source_update_candidates.title: tools/project.py controls wibo wrapper auto-selection
source_update_candidates.text: tools/project.py contains use_wibo logic for compiler wrapper selection. In PR #2417, wibo auto-use was restricted to Linux after macOS attempted to run build/tools/wibo and failed with 'cannot execute binary file'.
source_update_candidates.reason: May be useful as a path fact for future build-tooling PR triage, but should be curated against current source before promotion.

source_update_candidates.title: Build troubleshooting note for macOS wibo/wine confusion
source_update_candidates.text: Historical PR evidence shows macOS builds failed when auto-selecting build/tools/wibo instead of the intended wrapper path; the observed shell error was '/bin/sh: build/tools/wibo: cannot execute binary file'.
source_update_candidates.reason: Could inform developer setup or troubleshooting documentation if current tooling still has comparable wrapper-selection behavior.
